# Requirements: Does DSSYK Have a Semiclassical dS Bulk Dual?

**Defined:** 2026-04-09
**Core Research Question:** Does DSSYK have a semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual, and if so is it a full bulk dual or only a constrained-sector correspondence?

## Primary Requirements

### Derivations

- [ ] **DERV-01**: Extract the precise regime assumptions and observable dictionary used in the main DSSYK-to-dS proposals.
- [ ] **DERV-02**: State the minimum conditions for a full semiclassical bulk dual, including correlator, thermal, entropic, and operator-map consistency.

### Calculations

- [ ] **CALC-01**: Reconstruct the correlator evidence in arXiv:2310.16994 and arXiv:2402.02584 with explicit regime caveats.
- [ ] **CALC-02**: Reconstruct the temperature, entropy, and fake-temperature interpretation in arXiv:2209.09997 and arXiv:2404.03535.

### Validations

- [ ] **VALD-01**: Check whether any candidate bulk description passes both the correlator and thermal/dictionary tests.
- [ ] **VALD-02**: Distinguish full duality, constrained-sector duality, and unsupported simple semiclassical duality without relying on proxy evidence.
- [ ] **VALD-03**: Record at least one disconfirming route that would force the verdict away from a full dual.

## Follow-up Requirements

### Extensions

- **EXTN-01**: Compare the verdict against later chord and 3D de Sitter follow-up papers not covered in the first-pass anchor set.
- **EXTN-02**: Assess whether the verdict changes once nonperturbative or finite-N questions are included.

## Out of Scope

| Topic | Reason |
| ----- | ------ |
| New microscopic bulk construction | Not needed to answer whether the current literature already supports the claim. |
| Finite-N numerics | Outside the scope of this analytic first-pass audit. |

## Accuracy and Validation Criteria

| Requirement | Accuracy Target | Validation Method |
| ----------- | --------------- | ----------------- |
| CALC-01 | Exact regime and observable statement for each anchor paper | Re-read abstract and paper-level claims against the final report |
| CALC-02 | No silent collapse of fake temperature into ordinary thermality | Cross-paper consistency check with explicit caveats |
| VALD-01 | Every positive verdict must satisfy both correlator and thermal/dictionary checks | Anchor-by-anchor comparison |
| VALD-02 | Final classification must separate full and weaker correspondences | Human review against forbidden proxies |

## Contract Coverage

| Requirement | Decisive Output / Deliverable | Anchor / Benchmark / Reference | Prior Inputs / Baselines | False Progress To Reject |
| ----------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------ | ------------------------ |
| DERV-01 | Observable and regime ledger in the report | Ref-2209-09997, Ref-2310-16994, Ref-2402-02584, Ref-2404-03535 | Project contract anchor registry | Mixing regimes or conventions silently |
| CALC-01 | Correlator comparison section in the report | Ref-2310-16994, Ref-2402-02584 | Published correlator claims | Treating any formal resemblance as decisive |
| CALC-02 | Thermal-consistency section in the report | Ref-2209-09997, Ref-2404-03535 | Published thermal or fake-temperature discussion | Declaring thermality solved because a parameter is named temperature |
| VALD-01 | Final verdict section in the report | All four anchor papers | The full comparison built in earlier requirements | Correlator-only success |
| VALD-02 | Explicit status classification | All four anchor papers | Same as above | Ambiguous rhetoric that does not classify the claim |
